glm-5-turbo.description: Wrote tests referencing get_health_status() and latency_bucket() but actual API exports are get_model_health() and _determine_latency_bucket(). Test file unusable without full rewrite.
glm-5-turbo.rule: When assigning test-writing tasks, include exact function signatures from the module under test

glm-5-turbo.description: Worker reported success but produced zero file changes. No test_events.py was created despite clear task instructions.
glm-5-turbo.rule: For test tasks, prefer splitting into smaller scopes; glm-5-turbo may silently fail on larger test files

kimi-k2.5.description: Imported private function _emit_event instead of public emit_event. Called emit_event('started', {dict}) but actual signature is emit_event(type, model, *, note=None). Fallback function had wrong arity.
kimi-k2.5.rule: Include exact import statements and function signatures in task prompt when modifying files that call new modules

kimi-k2.5.description: Task specified 'only modify mms_launchers.py' but worker modified 4 files: mms_core.py, mms_tui.py, mms_router.py, mms_launchers.py. Also moved variable declarations between functions and used wrong module name mms_health instead of mms_health_cache.
kimi-k2.5.rule: Always include explicit 'ONLY modify these files: [list]' constraint. kimi-k2.5 aggressively expands scope.

kimi-k2.5.description: Used $TMPDIR/health-cache.json instead of ~/.config/mms/health-cache.json. Parsed .status at JSON root but actual structure is .records[model].status. Used wrong status enum values (healthy/unhealthy vs ok/slow/degraded/blocked).
kimi-k2.5.rule: When task involves reading JSON files, include the exact file path and a sample of the JSON structure in the task prompt

glm-5-turbo: Weak at cross-file API references: when writing tests or code that imports another module, MUST include exact function signatures (name, params, return type) in the task description
May produce zero output on complex tasks: prefer splitting into smaller scopes, each targeting 1 file with clear acceptance criteria
kimi-k2.5: Aggressively expands modification scope: ALWAYS include 'ONLY modify these files: [explicit list]' in task prompt. Will modify unrelated files if not constrained.
Wrong import paths when module names are similar: include exact 'from X import Y' statements in the task description
When reading/writing JSON or config files: include exact file path AND a sample of the JSON structure (not just field names)
